title: Wrong Health Plan Costs You $2,100 Every Year
hook: Fifty-one percent of workers pick the wrong health insurance plan every open enrollment — and the average mistake costs them over $2,100 a year without them ever realizing it.
angle: Status quo bias plus fear of the word 'deductible' keeps people in expensive plans, when a high-deductible plan paired with an HSA almost always wins the math for healthy workers.
audience: Anyone with employer health insurance who clicks re-enroll every year without running the actual numbers
key_numbers: 51% pick the wrong plan (Carnegie Mellon study)
HSA is triple tax-free — the only account with that benefit
$1,920/yr HDHP vs $3,840/yr PPO for the same coverage
$34,000 built in 10 years by investing the premium difference
